sanskrit: त इमेऽवस्थिता युद्धे प्राणांस्त्यक्त्वा धनानि च। आचार्याः पितरः पुत्रास्तथैव च पितामहाः॥
transliteration: ta ime ’vasthitā yuddhe prāṇāṁs tyaktvā dhanāni ca ācāryāḥ pitaraḥ putrās tathaiva ca pitāmahāḥ
literal_meaning: These very ones, having abandoned life and wealth, are stationed in battle—teachers, fathers, sons, and likewise grandfathers.
contextual_meaning: Arjuna’s mind catastrophizes the total loss of his relational world; every attachment figure is about to annihilate itself, making victory meaningless.
psychological_state: Acute stress reaction with anticipatory grief; ego fragments under the threat of total relational extinction.
trigger_scenario: A CEO about to sign off on mass layoffs that will include lifelong mentors, childhood friends and family members.
anchor_text: Hold steady in duty even when every beloved face stands on the battlefield.
